narrative.headline: What 2020 Nissan Altima owners complain about most
narrative.summary: Owners filed 171 complaints about the 2020 Nissan Altima with NHTSA. Electrical system problems lead the list at 24% of all reports, followed by engine and forward collision avoidance issues. A handful of reports mention a fire, crash, or injury, so the details below are worth reading.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 NISSAN ALTIMA. THE CONTACT STATED THAT WHILE DRIVING IN REVERSE THE AUTOMATIC ELECTRONIC BRAKING ACTIVATED ALTHOUGH NO OBJECTS WERE PRESENT BEHIND THE VEHICLE. ADDITIONALLY, WHILE SWITCHING TO THE REVERSE THE MESSAGE INDICATING AN OBJECT WAS BEHIND THE
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most-reported area, with 41 complaints (24% of all reports). Six of them involved a fire and one involved an injury.

issues.sample: DURING COLD TEMPERATURES THE CAR ACCELERATES POORLY, ENGINE RPM  IS UNUSUAL. AT LEAST ONE INSTANCE OF THE ENGINE WARNING LIGHT ILLUMINATES WITH A "SERVICE NOW" MESSAGE DISPLAYED. IN ALL CASES THE VEHICLE ENGINE WAS RUNNING, IN (SLOW) MOTION, AND ON A CITY STREET  AND ONE INCIDENT
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The second most-reported area, with 34 complaints (20% of all reports). Two involved a fire and one involved a crash.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 28 complaints (16% of all reports). Four of them involved a crash and one involved an injury.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 20 complaints (12% of all reports). Three of them involved a crash and two involved an injury.

issues.blurb: 17 complaints (10% of all reports), with no fires, crashes, or injuries reported.
